title: betula pubescens subsp. rhombifolia
intro: Betula pubescens subsp. rhombifolia, also known as the rhombic birch or white birch, is a subspecies of the Betula pubescens tree species, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and habitat adaptation. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Betula pubescens subsp. rhombifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal problems.
